codeReview.summary: Created 5 platform/operations flow packets (auth-identity, tenant-admin-management, catalog-setup-readiness, manufacturing-packing, inventory-management) totaling 1,232 lines of documentation across 6 files. All packets follow a consistent 10-section structure covering actors, entrypoints, preconditions, lifecycle, completion boundary/limitations, canonical vs non-canonical paths, cross-module dependencies, security considerations, related documentation, and open decisions. Each packet is behavior-first and code-grounded, with specific HTTP method/path/actor entries and explicit current limitations. FLOW-INVENTORY.md correctly updated to remove '(packet pending)' markers for flows 1–5.

issues.description: The Flows section still reads 'Individual flow packets for each family will be added in later milestones.' This text is now stale — 5 of the 11 flow families now have canonical packets. Should be updated to reflect current state, e.g., 'Five flow packets (flows 1–5) are now available; the remaining six will be added in later milestones.'

FLOW-INVENTORY.md freshness marker is 'Last reviewed: 2026-03-29' but the file was modified in this commit (2026-03-30). The marker should be bumped to 2026-03-30 to reflect the update that removed pending markers for flows 1–5.

summary: PASS with 2 non-blocking issues. The 5 platform/operations flow packets are well-structured, behavior-first, code-grounded, and explicitly document current limitations. All required sections are present in every packet. Cross-references point to existing module docs. The FLOW-INVENTORY.md correctly links to the new packets with pending markers removed. Two minor freshness/staleness issues: (1) INDEX.md prose claims flow packets haven't been created yet, which is now inaccurate for 5 of 11 families, and (2) FLOW-INVENTORY.md freshness marker is one day behind the actual modification date.
